what_it_does: Git ignore rules for the repo: excludes Python/Node build artifacts and venvs, runtime store data, per-user Obsidian workspace/cache state, generated CLI telemetry/logs, secret credential files (connector boundary env, CD verify drive creds, Tailscale Funnel TLS cert+key), and the-one-system regenerable scratch directories.
observations: Ignores runtime store dirs: store-data/, .data/ (commented 'written by the live system, never code')
Obsidian section keeps shared settings but ignores workspace.json, workspace-mobile.json, cache, graph.json
Secret files are heavily commented as live-out-of-tree, chmod 600, NEVER commit: .boundary.env, .cd-drive.*, .cd-history.*, and *.ts.net.key/.crt (named workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net.*)
Ignores build scratch (.build/), CLI telemetry (ops/cli/telemetry.jsonl), .adv.log, .loop.log, mcp_registration_debug.log
Ignores build-prep/the-one-system/discovery/ and fusion-scope/ as regenerable scratch
standouts: Explicit comments naming a live EC private key (*.ts.net.key) and a specific host workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net as TLS cert/key that must never be committed
Distinguishes 'written by the live system, never code' (.data/) from source — a code-vs-runtime boundary encoded in ignore rules
conventions: Uses descriptive comment blocks to explain WHY each secret/runtime path is ignored, not just listing patterns
Both glob (*.ts.net.key) and explicit-name (workstation001...) forms for the same secret class
concerns: Naming a specific Tailscale hostname (workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net) in a tracked file leaks an internal host identifier
notes: A heavily-annotated gitignore that doubles as documentation of the repo's secret files, runtime store boundary, and regenerable scratch.
questions: Are the secret files (boundary.env, TLS key) actually managed out-of-tree as the comments claim?
intention_signals: To keep the tracked tree free of secrets, runtime-written state, and regenerable scratch, while documenting the boundary so agents understand what is source versus generated.
novelty: The inline prose explaining each secret's nature (live EC private key, chmod 600 out-of-tree) is more documentary than a typical gitignore.
contribution: Contributes the precise exclusion boundary for secrets, venvs, runtime store, Obsidian per-user state, and the-one-system scratch.
summary_for_embedding: A .gitignore excluding venvs, runtime store data, Obsidian workspace state, logs, secret credentials, TLS cert/key, and the-one-system regenerable scratch.
intention_for_embedding: To exclude secrets, runtime-written state, and regenerable scratch from version control while documenting the code-vs-runtime boundary.
